mission.title: kagent: [FEATURE] Support for OAuth2/OpenID Connect Integration (Azure Entra ID, Keycloak, etc.) in Webchat
mission.description: [FEATURE] Support for OAuth2/OpenID Connect Integration (Azure Entra ID, Keycloak, etc.) in Webchat. Requested by 6+ users.

steps.title: Check current kagent deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kagent version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kagent -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagent
helm list -n kagent 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kagent installation.

steps.title: Review kagent configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kagent configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kagent -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagent
kubectl get configmap -n kagent -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kagent
```
### 📋 Prerequisites

### 📝 Feature Summary

We would like to request the implementation of a feature that allows the webchat to integrate with an external authentication and authorization server (e.g., OAuth2, OpenID Connect).

steps.title: Apply the fix for [FEATURE] Support for OAuth2/OpenID Connect Integration…
steps.description:
Adding your own security solution also adds a security risk compared to an already hardened existing one ( let's assume oauth2 proxy (or a sidecar of user's choice) is as such) 

>if is possible to integrate OAuth2 directly into the application itself, that is preferable. Doing so avoids introducing an additional component, 

Whether a mechanism is built-in or not, the additional component is introduced the same.
Further down the line, _not_ introducing the component opens the capability for

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kagent -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kagent
kubectl get events -n kagent --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[FEATURE] Support for OAuth2/OpenID Connect Integration…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Adding your own security solution also adds a security risk compared to an already hardened existing one ( let's assume oauth2 proxy (or a sidecar of user's choice) is as such) 

>if is possible to integrate OAuth2 directly into the application itself, that is preferable.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kagent installed or the issue environment reproducible.
